Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees Under Ccc 3426.4
Off Calendar Apr-10-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD I. SCHARLAT OF THE LOCKE & LORD LLP, TEL. NO. (212) 415-8510 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. E. JEFFREY BANCHERO & ATTY. JOHN D. ELLIS OF THE BANCHERO LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 398-7000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CAROLINE VENZA and MISSION CTRL COMMUNICATIONS' Motion For Reasonable Attorneys Fees Under Ccc 3426.4 is granted. The Plaintiff's claim for misappropriation of trade secrets was objectively specious and made in subjective bad faith. The Defendant is awarded attorneys'fees in the amount of $123,870.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2013 Set For Trial On Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Continued To Dec-04-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JOHN ELLIS, ESQ., 415.398.7000, FOR DEFENDANT CAROLINE VENZA; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs CAROLINE VENZA & MISSION CONTROL COMMUNICATIONS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 2 through 4. The only actionable conduct that plaintiff cites parrots the misappropriation cause of action. In other words, the common law and UCL causes of action are based on the same nucleus of facts as the misappropriation claim. Even if defendant's pre-resignation conduct is distinct from the misappropriation, plaintiff cannot rely on that conduct under the parties' release, which is attached to the AC. Plaintiff's interpretation of the separation agreement (that the fiduciary duty survived the resignation) is unreasonable. Such an interpretation would run afoul of B&P 16600. Defendant owed a continuing duty to protect plaintiff's trade secrets. Defendant does not owe a continuing duty to act as plaintiff's fiduciary. Order signed in open court. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
